an3.34:1.1: “Monks, these three are causes for the origination of actions.
an3.34:1.3: Greed is a cause for the origination of actions. Aversion is a cause for the origination of actions. Delusion is a cause for the origination of actions.
an3.34:2.1: “Any action performed with greed—born of greed, caused by greed, originating from greed: Wherever one’s selfhood [atta-bhāva] turns up, there that action will ripen.
an3.34:2.2: Where that action ripens, there one will experience its fruit, either in this very life that has arisen or further along in the sequence.
an3.34:3.1: “Any action performed with aversion—born of aversion, caused by aversion, originating from aversion: Wherever one’s selfhood turns up, there that action will ripen.
an3.34:3.2: Where that action ripens, there one will experience its fruit, either in this very life that has arisen or further along in the sequence.
an3.34:4.1: “Any action performed with delusion—born of delusion, caused by delusion, originating from delusion: Wherever one’s selfhood turns up, there that action will ripen.
an3.34:4.2: Where that action ripens, there one will experience its fruit, either in this very life that has arisen or further along in the sequence.
an3.34:5.1: “ Just as when seeds are not broken, not rotten, not damaged by wind & heat, capable of sprouting, well-buried, planted in well-prepared soil,
an3.34:5.2: and the rain-god would offer good streams of rain:
an3.34:5.3: Those seeds would thus come to growth, increase, & abundance.
an3.34:5.4: In the same way, any action performed with greed…
an3.34:6.2: performed with delusion—born of delusion, caused by delusion, originating from delusion: Wherever one’s selfhood turns up, there that action will ripen.
an3.34:6.3: Where that action ripens, there one will experience its fruit, either in this very life that has arisen or further along in the sequence.
an3.34:6.4: “These are three causes for the origination of actions.
an3.34:7.1: “Now, these three are (further) causes for the origination of actions.
an3.34:7.3: Non-greed is a cause for the origination of actions. Non-aversion is a cause for the origination of actions. Non-delusion is a cause for the origination of actions.
an3.34:8.1: “Any action performed with non-greed—born of non-greed, caused by non-greed, originating from non-greed: When greed is gone, that action is thus abandoned, its root destroyed, made like a palmyra stump, deprived of the conditions of development, not destined for future arising.
an3.34:9.1: “Any action performed with non-aversion—born of non-aversion, caused by non-aversion, originating from non-aversion: When aversion is gone, that action is thus abandoned, its root destroyed, made like a palmyra stump, deprived of the conditions of development, not destined for future arising.
an3.34:10.1: “Any action performed with non-delusion—born of non-delusion, caused by non-delusion, originating from non-delusion: When delusion is gone, that action is thus abandoned, its root destroyed, made like a palmyra stump, deprived of the conditions of development, not destined for future arising.
an3.34:11.1: “ Just as when seeds are not broken, not rotten, not damaged by wind & heat, capable of sprouting, well-buried, planted in well-prepared soil,
an3.34:11.2: and a man would burn them with fire and, burning them with fire,
an3.34:11.3: would make them into fine ashes.
an3.34:11.4: Having made them into fine ashes, he would winnow them before a high wind or wash them away in a swift-flowing stream.
an3.34:11.5: Those seeds would thus be destroyed at the root, made like a palmyra stump, deprived of the conditions of development, not destined for future arising.
an3.34:11.6: “In the same way, any action performed with non-greed…
an3.34:12.2: performed with non-delusion—born of non-delusion, caused by non-delusion, originating from non-delusion:
an3.34:12.3: When delusion is gone, that action is thus abandoned, its root destroyed, made like a palmyra stump, deprived of the conditions of development, not destined for future arising.
an3.34:12.4: “These, monks, are three causes for the origination of action.”
an3.34:13.1: A person unknowing: the actions performed by him, born of greed, born of aversion, & born of delusion,
an3.34:14.1: So a monk, knowing, sheds greed, aversion, & delusion;
an3.34:14.3: giving rise to clear knowledge, he
